title: Pad Thai
description: A classic Thai stir-fried noodle dish with a sweet, savory, and tangy sauce, topped with crushed peanuts.
steps: Soak noodles in warm water until soft, then drain.
Stir-fry shrimp or tofu in a wok until cooked, push to one side.
Scramble eggs on the empty side of the wok.
Add noodles, Pad Thai paste, and fish sauce. Toss everything together.
Mix in bean sprouts and peanuts. Serve hot with a squeeze of lime.

cookingTips: For the best Pad Thai, always use fresh, high-quality ingredients.
Do not rush the cooking process; allow the flavors to meld naturally.
Adjust the spices according to your personal taste preference.

faq.question: Can I make Pad Thai ahead of time?
faq.answer: Yes, you can prepare the ingredients in advance, but it's best served fresh.

faq.question: How do I store the leftovers?
faq.answer: Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

faq.question: Can I freeze this dish?
faq.answer: It is generally not recommended to freeze this as it may alter the texture.
